findings.title: Verification cache key may omit attacker-controlled input
findings.summary: The proof attempt cannot establish that all attacker-controlled fields are included in the cache key.

affectedLocations.description: Candidate key construction path from fixture audit.

proofAttempt.map: Relevant code paths include buildCacheKey and verification request parsing.
proofAttempt.prove: The implementation must show that every verification-affecting attacker-controlled field is included in the key material.
proofAttempt.stressTest: Counterexample candidate: two requests differ in an attacker-controlled field that affects verification but share the same cache key.
proofAttempt.report: Treat as a candidate until reviewed by the three-gate security review.

notes: Candidate finding is not a confirmed vulnerability.

findings.title: Test-only cache helper resembles production key construction
findings.summary: The proof attempt found similar test helper code, but the path may be outside the audit scope.

proofAttempt.map: Candidate code path appears only in fixture helper files.
proofAttempt.prove: The implementation would need to show the helper is reachable from production entry points.
proofAttempt.stressTest: No production entry point reaches the fixture helper in the MVP scope.
proofAttempt.report: Candidate is expected to be rejected by the scope gate.
